### `dataset_source`

integer | array of integer

IDB has several source datafiles. This field helps keep track of where a row came from originally.

Choices (10):

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `1` | Civil cases filed and terminated from SY 1970 through SY 1987 |
| `2` | Civil cases filed, terminated, and pending from SY 1988 to present (2017) |
| `8` | Civil cases filed, terminated, and pending from SY 1988 to present (2020) |
| `9` | Civil cases filed, terminated, and pending from SY 1988 to present (September 2021) |
| `10` | Civil cases filed, terminated, and pending from SY 1988 to present (March 2022) |
| `3` | Criminal defendants filed and terminated from SY 1970 through FY 1995 |
| `4` | Criminal defendants filed, terminated, and pending from FY 1996 to present (2017) |
| `5` | Appellate cases filed and terminated from SY 1971 through FY 2007 |
| `6` | Appellate cases filed, terminated, and pending from FY 2008 to present (2017) |
| `7` | Bankruptcy cases filed, terminated, and pending from FY 2008 to present (2017) |

### `date_created`

string (date-time)

The moment when the item was created.

Lookups: `day`, `gt`, `gte`, `hour`, `lt`, `lte`, `minute`, `month`, `range`, `second`, `year`

### `date_modified`

string (date-time)

The last moment when the item was modified. A value in year 1750 indicates the value is unknown

Lookups: `day`, `gt`, `gte`, `hour`, `lt`, `lte`, `minute`, `month`, `range`, `second`, `year`

### `circuit`

integer

Circuit in which the case was filed.

### `district`

integer

District court in which the case was filed.

### `origin`

integer | array of integer

A single digit code describing the manner in which the case was filed in the district.

<details><summary>Choices (13)</summary>

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `1` | Original Proceeding |
| `2` | Removed  (began in the state court, removed to the district court) |
| `3` | Remanded for further action (removal from court of appeals) |
| `4` | Reinstated/reopened (previously opened and closed, reopened for additional action) |
| `5` | Transferred from another district(pursuant to 28 USC 1404) |
| `6` | Multi district litigation (cases transferred to this district by an order entered by Judicial Panel on Multi District Litigation pursuant to 28 USC 1407) |
| `7` | Appeal to a district judge of a magistrate judge's decision |
| `8` | Second reopen |
| `9` | Third reopen |
| `10` | Fourth reopen |
| `11` | Fifth reopen |
| `12` | Sixth reopen |
| `13` | Multi district litigation originating in the district (valid beginning July 1, 2016) |

</details>

### `date_filed`

string (date)

The date on which the case was filed in the district.

Lookups: `day`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `month`, `range`, `year`

### `jurisdiction`

integer | array of integer

The code which provides the basis for the U.S. district court jurisdiction in the case. This code is used in conjunction with appropriate nature of suit code.

Choices (5):

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `1` | Government plaintiff |
| `2` | Government defendant |
| `3` | Federal question |
| `4` | Diversity of citizenship |
| `5` | Local question |

### `title`

string

No description provided by FJC.

Lookups: `iexact`, `istartswith`, `startswith`

### `section`

string

No description provided by FJC.

Lookups: `iexact`, `istartswith`, `startswith`

### `subsection`

string

No description provided by FJC.

Lookups: `iexact`, `istartswith`, `startswith`

### `arbitration_at_filing`

string | array of string

This field is used only by the courts  participating in the Formal Arbitration Program.  It is not used for any other purpose.

Choices (4):

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `"M"` | Mandatory |
| `"V"` | Voluntary |
| `"E"` | Exempt |
| `"Y"` | Yes, but type unknown |

### `arbitration_at_termination`

string | array of string

Termination arbitration code.

Choices (4):

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `"M"` | Mandatory |
| `"V"` | Voluntary |
| `"E"` | Exempt |
| `"Y"` | Yes, but type unknown |

### `class_action`

boolean

Involves an allegation by the plaintiff that the complaint meets the prerequisites of a "Class Action" as provided in Rule 23 - F.R.CV.P.

### `plaintiff`

string

First listed plaintiff. This field appears to be cut off at 30 characters

Lookups: `iexact`, `istartswith`, `startswith`

### `defendant`

string

First listed defendant. This field appears to be cut off at 30 characters.

Lookups: `iexact`, `istartswith`, `startswith`

### `termination_class_action_status`

integer | array of integer

A code that indicates a case involving allegations of class action.

Choices (2):

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `2` | Denied |
| `3` | Granted |

### `procedural_progress`

integer | array of integer

The point to which the case had progressed when it was disposed of. See notes in codebook.

<details><summary>Choices (13)</summary>

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `1` | Before issue joined: No court action (before issue joined) |
| `2` | Before issue joined: Order entered |
| `11` | Before issue joined: Hearing held |
| `12` | Before issue joined: Order decided |
| `3` | After issue joined: No court action (after issue joined) |
| `4` | After issue joined: Judgment on motion |
| `5` | After issue joined: Pretrial conference held |
| `6` | After issue joined: During court trial |
| `7` | After issue joined: During jury trial |
| `8` | After issue joined: After court trial |
| `9` | After issue joined: After jury trial |
| `10` | After issue joined: Other |
| `13` | After issue joined: Request for trial de novo after arbitration |

</details>

### `disposition`

integer | array of integer

The manner in which the case was disposed of.

<details><summary>Choices (21)</summary>

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `0` | Cases transferred or remanded: Transfer to another district |
| `1` | Cases transferred or remanded: Remanded to state court |
| `10` | Cases transferred or remanded: Multi-district litigation transfer |
| `11` | Cases transferred or remanded: Remanded to U.S. agency |
| `2` | Dismissals: Want of prosecution |
| `3` | Dismissals: Lack of jurisdiction |
| `12` | Dismissals: Voluntarily dismissed |
| `13` | Dismissals: Settled |
| `14` | Dismissals: Other |
| `4` | Judgment on: Default |
| `5` | Judgment on: Consent |
| `6` | Judgment on: Motion before trial |
| `7` | Judgment on: Jury verdict |
| `8` | Judgment on: Directed verdict |
| `9` | Judgment on: Court trial |
| `15` | Judgment on: Award of arbitrator |
| `16` | Judgment on: Stayed pending bankruptcy |
| `17` | Judgment on: Other |
| `18` | Judgment on: Statistical closing |
| `19` | Judgment on: Appeal affirmed (magistrate judge) |
| `20` | Judgment on: Appeal denied (magistrate judge |

</details>

### `judgment`

integer | array of integer

Which party the cases was disposed in favor of.

Choices (4):

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `1` | Plaintiff |
| `2` | Defendant |
| `3` | Both plaintiff and defendant |
| `4` | Unknown |

### `pro_se`

integer | array of integer

Which parties filed pro se? (See codebook for more details.)

Choices (4):

| Value | Label |
|---|---|
| `0` | No pro se plaintiffs or defendants |
| `1` | Pro se plaintiffs, but no pro se defendants |
| `2` | Pro se defendants, but no pro se plaintiffs |
| `3` | Both pro se plaintiffs & defendants |

string
